title: Organic Irregular Quad Trade Grid
description: Implement an Oskar-style irregular grid generator for the trade map using deterministic blue-noise point sampling, iterative Lloyd/CVT relaxation, and a quad-construction pass that produces playable tile topology. Integrate the generated grid into trade-map runtime flow so tile adjacency, placement, and route/pathing systems can consume it behind a controlled enablement path.
definition-of-done: Given a fixed seed and map bounds, grid generation is deterministic across runs. The generation pipeline executes in staged steps (sampling, relaxation, quad construction) and exports topology/adjacency data usable by trade-map systems. Produced meshes satisfy topology expectations (quad-first interior with explicitly handled boundary exceptions) and preserve connected, traversable tile adjacency. Runtime integration allows toggling or selecting the irregular grid path without regressing existing map behavior when disabled.
testing-plan: Run generation with multiple fixed seeds and verify identical output hashes/metrics per seed across repeated runs. Validate topology invariants on representative map sizes: expected interior quad ratio, no duplicate edges, no orphaned vertices, and consistent boundary handling. Execute integration checks that load generated grids into trade-map placement/pathing flows and verify adjacency traversal remains valid. Benchmark generation/runtime overhead on small, medium, and large map targets and confirm performance stays within agreed frame/load budgets.
todos: Implement deterministic blue-noise (or Poisson-disc) sampling with seed-controlled output for map bounds
Implement configurable Lloyd/CVT relaxation iterations and expose debug metrics per iteration
Implement quad-construction pipeline from relaxed Voronoi/dual structures with explicit boundary-case handling
Build topology validation utilities (quad ratio, edge/vertex integrity, connected adjacency graph checks)
Integrate irregular quad grid output into trade map tile/adjacency consumers behind a feature toggle or selection path
Add debug visualization hooks for sampled points, relaxed cells, and final quad topology
Add automated seed-regression and performance validation scenarios for representative map sizes
unknowns: Whether strict all-quad output is required or boundary non-quad exceptions are acceptable for gameplay
Target generation budget split between startup precompute and runtime updates for map edits
Preferred source of truth for topology data (mesh-first vs adjacency-graph-first) in downstream trade systems
How irregular grid enablement should be surfaced in dev tooling and future player-facing map settings
